channel.title: Untitled Linux Show (Video)
channel.itunes:author: TWiT
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons License - Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0 International - http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/
channel.itunes:subtitle: The week's hottest Linux news for desktop, gaming, and enterprise.
channel.itunes:summary:
The Untitled Linux Show covers the week's hottest Linux news for desktop, gaming, and even enterprise. ULS is the weekly update you don't want to miss, from the latest kernel development to the updates on your favorite apps! Each episode finishes with a killer command line tip from each host. You can join Club TWiT for $10 per month and get ad-free audio and video feeds for all our shows plus everything else the club offers...or get just this podcast ad-free for $5 per month.
New episodes are recorded live every Saturday in the Club TWiT Discord.
channel.description:
The Untitled Linux Show covers the week's hottest Linux news for desktop, gaming, and even enterprise. ULS is the weekly update you don't want to miss, from the latest kernel development to the updates on your favorite apps! Each episode finishes with a killer command line tip from each host. You can join Club TWiT for $10 per month and get ad-free audio and video feeds for all our shows plus everything else the club offers...or get just this podcast ad-free for $5 per month.
New episodes are recorded live every Saturday in the Club TWiT Discord.
channel.itunes:keywords: Linux, desktop, gaming, enterprise, TWiT, open source software

item.title: ULS 245: Not a Supernova - Firefox Nova, Gnome 40, & Age Verification Fallout
item.itunes:title: Not a Supernova - Firefox Nova, Gnome 40, & Age Verification Fallout
item.itunes:author: TWiT
item.itunes:subtitle: Firefox Nova, Gnome 40, & Age Verification Fallout
item.itunes:keywords: Linux, command line, Linux, age verification laws, California law, anti-cheat for Linux, EA Javelin, ubuntu, System76, Fedora, MidnightBSD, GNOME 50, wayland, Mutter, KDE, Wine 11.4, debian, Debian diversity, AI in open source, ARM boards, Armbian 26.2
item.description:
This week, there's more age verification fallout, everybody hates Ubuntu, and Wine releases 11.4. Linux From Scratch goes SystemD, Gnome is testing version 50, and Debian released a community update. Armbian releases 26.2, EA teases Linux Anti-Cheat for Linux, and Firefox Nova leaks as the visual Firefox refresh. For tips, we have Waydroid for Android on Linux, --follow for journalctl parsing, MusicBrainz Picard for managing tagging, and then a quick primer on Block and Character devices. You can see the show notes at https://bit.ly/4rZmDWd and have a great week!

Host: Jonathan Bennett

Co-Hosts: Jeff Massie, Ken McDonald, and Rob Campbell

Download or subscribe to Untitled Linux Show at https://twit.tv/shows/untitled-linux-show

Join Club TWiT for Ad-Free Podcasts!
Support what you love and get ad-free audio and video feeds, a members-only Discord, and exclusive content. Join today: https://twit.tv/clubtwit

Club TWiT members can discuss this episode and leave feedback in the Club TWiT Discord.
item.itunes:summary:

Armbian releases 26.2, EA teases Linux Anti-Cheat for Linux, and Firefox Nova leaks as the visual Firefox refresh. For tips, we have Waydroid for Android on Linux, --follow for journalctl parsing, MusicBrainz Picard for managing tagging, and then a quick primer on Block and Character devices. item.content:encoded:
This week, there's more age verification fallout, everybody hates Ubuntu, and Wine releases 11.4. Linux From Scratch goes SystemD, Gnome is testing version 50, and Debian released a community update. Armbian releases 26.2, EA teases Linux Anti-Cheat for Linux, and Firefox Nova leaks as the visual Firefox refresh. For tips, we have Waydroid for Android on Linux, --follow for journalctl parsing, MusicBrainz Picard for managing tagging, and then a quick primer on Block and Character devices. You can see the show notes at https://bit.ly/4rZmDWd and have a great week!

Host: Jonathan Bennett

Co-Hosts: Jeff Massie, Ken McDonald, and Rob Campbell

media:content.media:title: ULS 245: Not a Supernova - Firefox Nova, Gnome 40, & Age Verification Fallout
media:content.media:description: Firefox Nova, Gnome 40, & Age Verification Fallout
media:content.media:keywords: Linux, command line, Linux, age verification laws, California law, anti-cheat for Linux, EA Javelin, ubuntu, System76, Fedora, MidnightBSD, GNOME 50, wayland, Mutter, KDE, Wine 11.4, debian, Debian diversity, AI in open source, ARM boards, Armbian 26.2

item.title: ULS 244: Torture the Metaphor - Age Verification, Git Redirects, & Security Changes
item.itunes:title: Torture the Metaphor - Age Verification, Git Redirects, & Security Changes
item.itunes:author: TWiT
item.itunes:subtitle: Age Verification, Git Redirects, & Security Changes
item.itunes:keywords: Linux, command line, Lenovo Legion Tab 3 review, android tablet, Termux, Android apocalypse, age verification legislation, sudo rs, rust, asterisk password feedback, Collabora Office, LibreOffice Online, open source UI design, laptop benchmarking
item.description:
This show starts with an Android review, looking at Jonathan's newest tablet. It also covers the coming Android apocalypse, the age verification legislation, and the sudo-rs asterisk fight. Mesa is grappling with AI, Ardour has a couple of point releases, and Gnome is redirecting traffic to GitHub. Fedora has a new mobile experiment in PocketBlue, and the 0 A.D. game has a stable release. For tips we have PyNetscan for IP scanning, snapper for BTRFS snapshots, mediainfo for media file investigations, and espanso for automatic text expansion. Find the show notes at https://bit.ly/3N9X3ys and enjoy!

Host: Jonathan Bennett

Co-Hosts: Ken McDonald, Rob Campbell, and Jeff Massie

Download or subscribe to Untitled Linux Show at https://twit.tv/shows/untitled-linux-show

Join Club TWiT for Ad-Free Podcasts!
Support what you love and get ad-free audio and video feeds, a members-only Discord, and exclusive content. Join today: https://twit.tv/clubtwit

Club TWiT members can discuss this episode and leave feedback in the Club TWiT Discord.

Sponsor:

  • bitwarden.com/twit

item.title: ULS 243: Only a Few Things Crashed - Intel is Back, Matrix is Out, and AI is Speeding up the Kernel
item.itunes:title: Only a Few Things Crashed - Intel is Back, Matrix is Out, and AI is Speeding up the Kernel
item.itunes:author: TWiT
item.itunes:subtitle: Intel is Back, Matrix is Out, and AI is Speeding up the Kernel
item.itunes:keywords: immutable Gentoo, MatrixOS, digital sovereignty, Red Hat Digital Sovereignty Readiness Assessment, KDE Plasma 6.6, live KDE upgrade, Blender 5.1, hardware ray tracing, Pipewire 1.6, Linux kernel 6.19.3, Clear Linux, Intel Linux jobs
item.description:
This week we cover an Immutable Gentoo-derived distro, the release of KDE 6.6, Blender 5.1 and PipeWire 1.6. Then Intel hires more Linux developers, with a very specific gaming-centric experience wishlist, and the kernel 6.19.3 minor update is out, fixing some serious issues. For tips we have whosthere for network device discovery, rate-mirrors for exploring update mirror speeds, system-ac-power for detecting power and battery state, and unzip for .zip handling. You can find the show notes at https://bit.ly/4rCL0ZK and enjoy!

Host: Jonathan Bennett

Co-Hosts: Jeff Massie, Rob Campbell, and Ken McDonald

Download or subscribe to Untitled Linux Show at https://twit.tv/shows/untitled-linux-show

Join Club TWiT for Ad-Free Podcasts!
Support what you love and get ad-free audio and video feeds, a members-only Discord, and exclusive content. Join today: https://twit.tv/clubtwit

Club TWiT members can discuss this episode and leave feedback in the Club TWiT Discord.

media:content.media:description: Intel is Back, Matrix is Out, and AI is Speeding up the Kernel

item.title: ULS 242: Syntactical Sugar - Sustainable Distros, New Kernels, & Wayland Vim?
item.itunes:title: Syntactical Sugar - Sustainable Distros, New Kernels, & Wayland Vim?
item.itunes:author: TWiT
item.itunes:subtitle: Sustainable Distros, New Kernels, & Wayland Vim?
item.itunes:keywords: Linux, command line, Linux Mint, pearOS, wayland, modular window managers, River Project, Linux kernel 6.19, Linux kernel 7.0, Mesa 26, cinnamon desktop, KDE, GNOME, Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, Rust in Linux kernel, IPFire DBL, domain block list, command line tips,
item.description:
The Linux Kernel 6.19 is out, the Rust experiment is over, and it's time to talk about 7.0. Vim 9.2 is out, with a bit of a weird new feature in its changelogs, and IPFire is an intriguing, community-driven security domain block list. PearOS has a new release for those seeking an Apple-inspired Linux experience, and Linux Mint is adjusting its release schedule to better manage developer and tester schedules. River is a new project trying to do Wayland support with a modular Desktop stack, and Mesa 26.0 is out with impressive performance gains. For tips, we have cull for finding and deleting big files, a systemd program for detecting if the OS is running virtualized, preload for caching applications in ram, and new_script for a script-writing tool that *doesn't* feature an LLM. You can find the show notes at https://bit.ly/3ZCNcEc and happy Linux'ing!

Host: Jonathan Bennett

Co-Hosts: Rob Campbell, Ken McDonald, and Jeff Massie

Download or subscribe to Untitled Linux Show at https://twit.tv/shows/untitled-linux-show

Join Club TWiT for Ad-Free Podcasts!
Support what you love and get ad-free audio and video feeds, a members-only Discord, and exclusive content. Join today: https://twit.tv/clubtwit

Club TWiT members can discuss this episode and leave feedback in the Club TWiT Discord.

Vim 9.2 is out, with a bit of a weird new feature in its changelogs, and IPFire is an intriguing, community-driven security domain block list. PearOS has a new release for those seeking an Apple-inspired Linux experience, and Linux Mint is adjusting its release schedule to better manage developer and tester schedules. River is a new project trying to do Wayland support with a modular Desktop stack, and Mesa 26.0 is out with impressive performance gains. For tips, we have cull for finding and deleting big files, a systemd program for detecting if the OS is running virtualized, preload for caching applications in ram, and new_script for a script-writing tool that *doesn't* feature an LLM. item.content:encoded:
The Linux Kernel 6.19 is out, the Rust experiment is over, and it's time to talk about 7.0. Vim 9.2 is out, with a bit of a weird new feature in its changelogs, and IPFire is an intriguing, community-driven security domain block list. PearOS has a new release for those seeking an Apple-inspired Linux experience, and Linux Mint is adjusting its release schedule to better manage developer and tester schedules. River is a new project trying to do Wayland support with a modular Desktop stack, and Mesa 26.0 is out with impressive performance gains. For tips, we have cull for finding and deleting big files, a systemd program for detecting if the OS is running virtualized, preload for caching applications in ram, and new_script for a script-writing tool that *doesn't* feature an LLM. You can find the show notes at https://bit.ly/3ZCNcEc and happy Linux'ing!

media:content.media:description: Sustainable Distros, New Kernels, & Wayland Vim?
media:content.media:keywords: Linux, command line, Linux Mint, pearOS, wayland, modular window managers, River Project, Linux kernel 6.19, Linux kernel 7.0, Mesa 26, cinnamon desktop, KDE, GNOME, Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, Rust in Linux kernel, IPFire DBL, domain block list, command line tips,

item.title: ULS 241: A Very Hot Sandwich - A Smorgasbord of New Releases
item.itunes:title: A Very Hot Sandwich - A Smorgasbord of New Releases
item.itunes:author: TWiT
item.itunes:subtitle: A Smorgasbord of New Releases
item.itunes:keywords: Linux, command line, LibreOffice 26.2, GIMP 3.2, Krita 6, Raspberry Pi price increase, dual RAM Raspberry Pi 4, Argon One laptop, open source office suite, GNU Coreutils 9.10, AI contributions, Bug Bounty, TLDraw, Debian community, LLM scrapers
item.description:
This week, we start by talking about the Raspberry Pi memory price increases and bemoan that it's a tough time to be an enthusiast. Then we help ourselves feel better by covering all the new Betas and releases of our favorite software. There's a new LibreOffice, a look ahead at GIMP 3.2, and the Krita 6 Beta. Toyota has announced Flourite, a new game engine written in Flutter and Dart. And Ardour 9 and Shotcut 26.1 are out. We talk Debian, and spend some time looking at how AI has changed the Open Source landscape. For tips, there's another look at systemd-analyze and then a quick intro to gpioget for reading gpio lines. You can find the show notes at https://bit.ly/4r3PmZn and have a great week!

Host: Jonathan Bennett

Co-Host: Ken McDonald

Download or subscribe to Untitled Linux Show at https://twit.tv/shows/untitled-linux-show

Join Club TWiT for Ad-Free Podcasts!
Support what you love and get ad-free audio and video feeds, a members-only Discord, and exclusive content. Join today: https://twit.tv/clubtwit

Club TWiT members can discuss this episode and leave feedback in the Club TWiT Discord.

Then we help ourselves feel better by covering all the new Betas and releases of our favorite software. There's a new LibreOffice, a look ahead at GIMP 3.2, and the Krita 6 Beta. Toyota has announced Flourite, a new game engine written in Flutter and Dart. And Ardour 9 and Shotcut 26.1 are out. We talk Debian, and spend some time looking at how AI has changed the Open Source landscape. For tips, there's another look at systemd-analyze and then a quick intro to gpioget for reading gpio lines. You can find the show notes at https://bit.ly/4r3PmZn and have a great week!

Then we help ourselves feel better by covering all the new Betas and releases of our favorite software. There's a new LibreOffice, a look ahead at GIMP 3.2, and the Krita 6 Beta. Toyota has announced Flourite, a new game engine written in Flutter and Dart. And Ardour 9 and Shotcut 26.1 are out. We talk Debian, and spend some time looking at how AI has changed the Open Source landscape. For tips, there's another look at systemd-analyze and then a quick intro to gpioget for reading gpio lines. You can find the show notes at https://bit.ly/4r3PmZn and have a great week!

media:content.media:keywords: Linux, command line, LibreOffice 26.2, GIMP 3.2, Krita 6, Raspberry Pi price increase, dual RAM Raspberry Pi 4, Argon One laptop, open source office suite, GNU Coreutils 9.10, AI contributions, Bug Bounty, TLDraw, Debian community, LLM scrapers

item.title: ULS 240: I Like the Frosting - We Review, We Work, & We Game
item.itunes:title: I Like the Frosting - We Review, We Work, & We Game
item.itunes:author: TWiT
item.itunes:subtitle: We Review, We Work, & We Game
item.itunes:keywords: Linux, command line, Argon 40 one up, Raspberry Pi CM5 laptop, Calibre ebook reader, GParted, AMD EPYC CPU, Intel Xeon Granite Rapids, GeForce Now Linux, KDE desktop, KDE VR mode, Plasma 6.7 Air theme, Cosmic desktop environment, XFCE Wayland compositor,
item.description:
This week starts with a quick review of the Argon40 One Up, the Laptop shell made for the Raspberry Pi CM5. Then we talk about the Calibre e-book reader, a shoot-out between AMD Epyc and Intel Xeon, and talk about the latest in desktop environments. Gparted ships an update, Proton and GeForce Now brings the Linux gaming, and the Raspberry Pi Display module is just begging to be rack mounted. For tips we have Libcaca for graphics in the terminal, A candy hack for pacman, and Sheelbeats for terminal music listening. You can find the show notes at https://bit.ly/4a8jVWD and enjoy!

Host: Jonathan Bennett

Co-Hosts: Ken McDonald and Jeff Massie

Download or subscribe to Untitled Linux Show at https://twit.tv/shows/untitled-linux-show

Join Club TWiT for Ad-Free Podcasts!
Support what you love and get ad-free audio and video feeds, a members-only Discord, and exclusive content. Join today: https://twit.tv/clubtwit

Club TWiT members can discuss this episode and leave feedback in the Club TWiT Discord.

Sponsor:

  • bitwarden.com/twit

item.title: ULS 239: Terrible at Metaphors - Photoshop, Linux Phones, & NVMe
item.itunes:title: Terrible at Metaphors - Photoshop, Linux Phones, & NVMe
item.itunes:author: TWiT
item.itunes:subtitle: Photoshop, Linux Phones, & NVMe
item.itunes:keywords: Linux, command line, Photoshop on Linux, Adobe Creative Cloud installer, WINE patches, NVMe Linux performance, kernel patch, OpenSUSE Merlin package manager, Graphite 2D editor, Linux phones, Next Phone, convergence phone, Android Linux Windows dual boot,
item.description:
Adobe Photoshop finally makes big progress on Linux, and the team unpacks what this means for creative pros, open-source rivals, and anyone dreaming of ditching Windows for good. Canonical's Snaps are under fire as scammers take malware tactics to a new level, hijacking trusted developer accounts and exposing a major risk for anyone installing crypto wallet apps on Linux. Find the show notes at https://bit.ly/4sZbOEk and have a great week!

Host: Jonathan Bennett

Co-Hosts: Ken McDonald, Jeff Massie, and Rob Campbell

Download or subscribe to Untitled Linux Show at https://twit.tv/shows/untitled-linux-show

Join Club TWiT for Ad-Free Podcasts!
Support what you love and get ad-free audio and video feeds, a members-only Discord, and exclusive content. Join today: https://twit.tv/clubtwit

Club TWiT members can discuss this episode and leave feedback in the Club TWiT Discord.
item.itunes:summary:
Adobe Photoshop finally makes big progress on Linux, and the team unpacks what this means for creative pros, open-source rivals, and anyone dreaming of ditching Windows for good. Canonical's Snaps are under fire as scammers take malware tactics to a new level, hijacking trusted developer accounts and exposing a major risk for anyone installing crypto wallet apps on Linux. Find the show notes at https://bit.ly/4sZbOEk and have a great week!

Host: Jonathan Bennett

Co-Hosts: Ken McDonald, Jeff Massie, and Rob Campbell

Download or subscribe to Untitled Linux Show at https://twit.tv/shows/untitled-linux-show

Join Club TWiT for Ad-Free Podcasts!
Support what you love and get ad-free audio and video feeds, a members-only Discord, and exclusive content. Join today: https://twit.tv/clubtwit

Club TWiT members can discuss this episode and leave feedback in the Club TWiT Discord.
item.content:encoded:
Adobe Photoshop finally makes big progress on Linux, and the team unpacks what this means for creative pros, open-source rivals, and anyone dreaming of ditching Windows for good. Canonical's Snaps are under fire as scammers take malware tactics to a new level, hijacking trusted developer accounts and exposing a major risk for anyone installing crypto wallet apps on Linux. Find the show notes at https://bit.ly/4sZbOEk and have a great week!

Host: Jonathan Bennett

Co-Hosts: Ken McDonald, Jeff Massie, and Rob Campbell

Download or subscribe to Untitled Linux Show at https://twit.tv/shows/untitled-linux-show

media:content.media:title: ULS 239: Terrible at Metaphors - Photoshop, Linux Phones, & NVMe
media:content.media:keywords: Linux, command line, Photoshop on Linux, Adobe Creative Cloud installer, WINE patches, NVMe Linux performance, kernel patch, OpenSUSE Merlin package manager, Graphite 2D editor, Linux phones, Next Phone, convergence phone, Android Linux Windows dual boot,

item.title: ULS 238: More Time to Bake - Pi AI, Wine 11, & IP Certs
item.itunes:title: More Time to Bake - Pi AI, Wine 11, & IP Certs
item.itunes:author: TWiT
item.itunes:subtitle: Pi AI, Wine 11, & IP Certs
item.itunes:keywords: Linux, command line, AI in Linux kernel, Linus Torvalds, Wine 11, Hangover 11, ARM64 Linux, Raspberry Pi AI Hat 2, PipeWire 1.4.10, OBS Studio 32 beta, wayland migration, Budgie desktop, Fedora Games Lab, EndeavourOS Ganymede Neo, Nvidia drivers Linux
item.description:
This week we're talking about Torvalds vibe coding, the newest Pi AI hat, and what's new with PipeWire and OBS Studio. Then there's some great news in Wine 11 and Hangover 11, Fedora's Games Spin is moving to KDE, and we talk about LetsEncrypt and their new IP certificates. For tips we have csvi for editing comma-separated-values files on the command line, espeak-ng for giving your Linux machine a voice, and the hidden support for Screen Savers in KDE. You can find the show notes at https://bit.ly/4jKwHyV and have a great week!

Host: Jonathan Bennett

Co-Hosts: Ken McDonald and Rob Campbell

Download or subscribe to Untitled Linux Show at https://twit.tv/shows/untitled-linux-show

Join Club TWiT for Ad-Free Podcasts!
Support what you love and get ad-free audio and video feeds, a members-only Discord, and exclusive content. Join today: https://twit.tv/clubtwit

Club TWiT members can discuss this episode and leave feedback in the Club TWiT Discord.

Then there's some great news in Wine 11 and Hangover 11, Fedora's Games Spin is moving to KDE, and we talk about LetsEncrypt and their new IP certificates. For tips we have csvi for editing comma-separated-values files on the command line, espeak-ng for giving your Linux machine a voice, and the hidden support for Screen Savers in KDE. This week we're talking about Torvalds vibe coding, the newest Pi AI hat, and what's new with PipeWire and OBS Studio. Then there's some great news in Wine 11 and Hangover 11, Fedora's Games Spin is moving to KDE, and we talk about LetsEncrypt and their new IP certificates. For tips we have csvi for editing comma-separated-values files on the command line, espeak-ng for giving your Linux machine a voice, and the hidden support for Screen Savers in KDE. item.title: ULS 237: Get Better Mice - AI, Arm, & Liquorix
item.itunes:title: Get Better Mice - AI, Arm, & Liquorix
item.itunes:author: TWiT
item.itunes:subtitle: AI, Arm, & Liquorix
item.itunes:keywords: Linux, command line, Linus Torvalds AI rant, Linux Foundation finances, kernel bug tracking, Budgie 10.10 Wayland migration, Unreal Engine 5 ray tracing, RADV driver update, Steam on ARM64, FEX emulator, Cosmic desktop, System76, Pop!_OS
item.description:
The kernel is ground zero for AI coding wars, and Torvalds is ranting about it. The Linux foundation made good use of their funds last year, and the average kernel bug has a surprisingly long life. Then we talk about the possible death of the middle-click, Steam and ARM64, and the liquorix kernel. There's a budgie release and big Raytracing performance uplift! For tips we have the fresh text editor, alsa-info, and clear! You can find the show notes at https://bit.ly/3N79tHc and Happy linuxing!

Host: Jonathan Bennett

Co-Hosts: Ken McDonald and Jeff Massie

Download or subscribe to Untitled Linux Show at https://twit.tv/shows/untitled-linux-show

Join Club TWiT for Ad-Free Podcasts!
Support what you love and get ad-free audio and video feeds, a members-only Discord, and exclusive content. Join today: https://twit.tv/clubtwit

Club TWiT members can discuss this episode and leave feedback in the Club TWiT Discord.

Then we talk about the possible death of the middle-click, Steam and ARM64, and the liquorix kernel. There's a budgie release and big Raytracing performance uplift! Then we talk about the possible death of the middle-click, Steam and ARM64, and the liquorix kernel. There's a budgie release and big Raytracing performance uplift! item.title: ULS 236: Still Waking Up - Accounting, Court Cases, & Space
item.itunes:title: Still Waking Up - Accounting, Court Cases, & Space
item.itunes:author: TWiT
item.itunes:subtitle: Accounting, Court Cases, & Space
item.itunes:keywords: Linux, command line, Linux accounting software, GnuCash 5.14, open source finance tools, QEMU virtualization, linux gaming, Steam survey Linux share, arch linux, Linux Mint, Ubuntu Core Snap, Vizio GPL lawsuit, Software Freedom Conservancy
item.description:
This week, we're talking accounting... while not giving any financial advice. Then we talk QEMU and virtualization, the Linux Steam Survey, and the CachyOS server. Then, Torvalds isn't super happy with the SFC lawsuit, Linux is headed to space, and Phoenix attempts to resurrect the ashes of X Server. You can find the show notes at https://bit.ly/49qpcbN and happy new year!

Host: Jonathan Bennett

Co-Hosts: Ken McDonald and Rob Campbell

Download or subscribe to Untitled Linux Show at https://twit.tv/shows/untitled-linux-show

Join Club TWiT for Ad-Free Podcasts!
Support what you love and get ad-free audio and video feeds, a members-only Discord, and exclusive content. Join today: https://twit.tv/clubtwit

Club TWiT members can discuss this episode and leave feedback in the Club TWiT Discord.

Sponsor:

  • bitwarden.com/twit
